mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically compressing valuation multiples and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in labor market conditions signals an imminent economic slowdown, triggering risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in > 60% of triggered instances despite the unemployment threshold breach, or the signal exhibits no predictive power in rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent industrial production expansion and housing permit contraction indicate a supply-led growth regime where corporate margins expand without inflationary pressure, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a baseline buy-and-hold strategy in out-of-sample testing, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer price inflation erodes real corporate earnings and forces prolonged monetary tightening, depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive or neutral during periods of accelerating CPI, or the historical correlation between CPI acceleration and SPX 60-day returns flips to positive in the most recent 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising real personal savings and consumer spending capacity provide a direct liquidity buffer that supports risk asset allocation, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal exhibits a negative or zero directional consistency over a 3-year rolling window, or forward returns do not significantly differ from the unconditional mean (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sharp contraction in industrial output breaches a critical supply threshold, indicating capacity constraints and input cost shocks that compress equity valuations and yield negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX forward returns are not systematically negative when INDPRO breaches the 100 level, or the signal's information coefficient drops below 0.02 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous payroll growth and unemployment decline confirm a self-reinforcing labor demand cycle that sustains corporate revenue growth, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to generate positive risk-adjusted returns in out-of-sample testing, or the partial correlation between the proxy pair and forward SPX returns becomes statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating real GDP growth following a recessionary trough signals a demand-driven earnings rebound, triggering positive S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain negative or flat during confirmed GDP acceleration phases, or the signal's predictive accuracy falls below 50% in a rolling 2-year evaluation window.
